name: Easter Paska
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 cups Flour (1 cup for flouring board)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve sugar in milk and heat until lukewarm.
2. Crumble yeast into milk and let stand. Sift flour and salt into a large bowl or pot.
3. Add eggs, melted vegetable shortening, and butter to the dry ingredients. Mix until all dough is in one loose mass, not sticky to the hands or sides of the bowl.
4. Cover with a damp cloth and set in a warm place to rise. The dough should double in size within 2-2.5 hours.
5. Take three large balls of dough, about the size of grapefruits, and form into 10-inch strips that are 3 inches wide.
6. Braid the strips together. Paska is always baked in a round pan, 6 inches high.
7. Bake at 400°F for the first 10 minutes, then lower to 375°F for the next 40-50 minutes.
8. Spread melted butter over the paska when removed from the oven. This will make 4 Paskas.
